title: GCC Morning Brief

sections.headline: GCC Markets Open Higher on Oil Stability
sections.summary: Major GCC indices opened in positive territory as oil prices held steady above $85 per barrel, boosting investor confidence across the region.

sections.headline: Saudi Arabia Announces New Tourism Visa Reforms
sections.summary: The Saudi Ministry of Tourism unveiled streamlined visa procedures for 50+ nationalities, aiming to attract 150 million annual visits by 2030.

sections.headline: UAE Central Bank Holds Interest Rates Steady
sections.summary: The UAE Central Bank maintained its base rate at 4.40%, aligning with the Federal Reserve's pause, to support economic growth amid global uncertainty.

sections.headline: Qatar Energy Signs Long-Term LNG Deal with Germany
sections.summary: QatarEnergy inked a 20-year agreement to supply 2 million tonnes of LNG annually to German utility companies, strengthening energy ties.

sections.headline: Kuwait's Non-Oil GDP Grows 3.2% in Q1
sections.summary: Kuwait's non-oil sector expanded 3.2% year-on-year in the first quarter, driven by construction and financial services, according to official data.

sections.headline: Oman Launches Green Hydrogen Tender Round 2
sections.summary: Oman's Ministry of Energy and Minerals opened bids for four new green hydrogen projects, targeting 1 million tonnes annual production by 2030.

sections.headline: Bahrain Fintech Ecosystem Attracts $50M in VC Funding
sections.summary: Bahrain's fintech startups raised $50 million in Q1 2025, a 40% increase year-on-year, driven by regulatory sandbox initiatives.

key_events: OPEC+ monthly meeting scheduled for April 15
Saudi GDP data release for Q1 2025 due next week
UAE announces new foreign direct investment law
